title: Dryoathyrium parvisorum
uses: Dryoathyrium parvisorum is a species of fern that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. taxonomy: Dryoathyrium parvisorum is classified within the family Dryopteridaceae, a family of ferns commonly known as wood ferns. This classification places it in the order Polypodiales, a group of ferns that are often found in shaded, woodland environments. The genus Dryoathyrium is typically associated with species that have leathery fronds and a tropical or subtropical distribution. Further classification of Dryoathyrium parvisorum may involve its placement within a specific section or series within the genus.
